channel.title: Naija Digest Online
channel.description: Nigeria’s one-stop hub for breaking news, tech, football, celebrity gossip, Naija music, live TV, exchange rates and trending topics.

item.title: NAFDAC moves to lift international restrictions on Nigerian beans
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
NAFDAC is strengthening food safety to lift international restrictions on Nigerian beans, enhancing export competitiveness and protecting consumers from pe
Read More: https://punchng.com/nafdac-moves-to-lift-international-restrictions-on-nigerian-beans/

item.title: Police recover diverted goods valued N32m in Oyo, arrest two suspects
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Oyo State Police Command recovers N32 million worth of diverted goods, including 570 bags of guinea corn, and arrests two suspects in Oyo town.
Read More: https://punchng.com/police-recover-diverted-goods-valued-n32m-in-oyo-arrest-two-suspects/

item.title: EU unveils €70m security, agriculture support for Nigeria
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
The European Union announces €70m support for Nigeria, including €45m for security and peacebuilding and €25m for agriculture. This strengthens the EU-Nige
Read More: https://punchng.com/eu-unveils-e70m-security-agriculture-support-for-nigeria/

item.title: Osun lawmaker dumps APC, emerges Wike-backed PDP senatorial candidate
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Osun Senator Olubiyi Fadeyi dumps APC for PDP, citing trust issues and unfair primaries. He emerges as the Wike-backed PDP senatorial candidate for 2027.
Read More: https://punchng.com/osun-lawmaker-dumps-apc-emerges-wike-backed-pdp-senatorial-candidate/

item.title: Oyo lawmaker unveils over N280m learning hubs, targets nine LGs
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Oyo lawmaker Remi Oseni launches N280m smart learning hubs across nine LGAs, offering AI, robotics, coding, and free exam registrations to bridge the digit
Read More: https://punchng.com/oyo-lawmaker-unveils-over-n280m-learning-hubs-targets-nine-lgs/

item.title: Only defeat or injury can stop Joshua-Fury clash — Hearn
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Eddie Hearn insists only defeat or injury can stop the long-awaited Anthony Joshua vs. Tyson Fury fight, dismissing Fury’s recent comments. Get the latest
Read More: https://punchng.com/only-defeat-or-injury-can-stop-joshua-fury-clash-hearn/

item.title: South-West CAN Opposes FRSC Bill Seeking To Sanction Preaching In Buses
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: ‎The Christian Association of Nigeria (CAN), South West Region, has urged President Bola Tinubu to withhold assent to the proposed Federal Road Safety Corps (FRSC) Act (Amendment) Bill, over provisions seeking to sanction preaching and prohibit hawking in commercial buses. ‎The association who said

item.title: Deadly sick Awa Bamiji appeals to Tinubu for overseas medical treatment
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: He disclosed that he has yet to regain his normal speech, cannot walk long distances without difficulty, and continues to suffer from severe tremors that impair his ability to write or use electronic devices.

item.title: FG begins training for 5,000 smart meter installers nationwide
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
The Federal Government has commenced training for 5,000 smart meter installers nationwide, a move set to boost electricity access and create jobs for Niger
Read More: https://punchng.com/fg-begins-training-for-5000-smart-meter-installers-nationwide/

item.title: Focus On Getting Nigeria Seat On UN Security Council, Tinubu Tells Jimoh Ibrahim
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: President Bola Tinubu has charged Nigeria’s Permanent Representative to the United Nations, Senator Jimoh Ibrahim, to focus on securing a seat for the country on the UN Security Council. The President gave the charge on Thursday when Ibrahim led a delegation of the research group, Aseyori Tinu

item.title: Obi meets British envoy, discusses democracy, economy, credible elections
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Peter Obi discusses Nigeria’s democracy, economy, and credible elections with British High Commissioner Richard Montgomery as his tenure concludes.
Read More: https://punchng.com/obi-meets-british-envoy-discusses-democracy-economy-credible-elections/

item.title: ADC tells Tinubu: Trump’s letter won’t determine Nigerians’ verdict
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: The opposition party accused the Tinubu administration of seeking international approval while many Nigerians continue to face economic hardship, insecurity and declining living conditions. The post ADC tells Tinubu: Trump’s letter won’t determine Nigerians’ verdict appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.title: PHOTO STORY: Lagos streets, bus stops become refuse dumps as collection system falters
item.dc:creator: Premium Times
item.description: Many parts of Lagos have continued to experience the pile-up of refuse despite Governor Sanwo-Olu's recent directive for scaled-up waste evacuation across the state. The post PHOTO STORY: Lagos streets, bus stops become refuse dumps as collection system falters appeared first on Premium Times Nigeri

item.title: Upwork niche targeting: Why ‘notion setup experts’ are billing $60 per hour
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: A few years ago, most freelancers on Upwork competed for generic jobs like virtual assistance, data entry, and content writing. Today, the marketplace looks very different. The post Upwork niche targeting: Why ‘notion setup experts’ are billing $60 per hour appeared first on Tribune Online.

The induction celebrated the successful transiti

item.title: Attacks: Plateau LG bans unauthorised herders’ settlements
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Qua’an-Pan Local Government in Plateau State bans unauthorised herders’ settlements and grazing on farmlands to boost security and peace.

item.title: US vows to punish Iran after tankers strike
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
The US vows to punish Iran after Houthi allies strike Saudi tankers, sending oil prices soaring and raising global alarm over escalating US Iran conflict.

item.description: Nervous investors set the stage for massive post-earnings moves in Wall Street’s biggest stocks.

item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: Withdrawal is latest instance of government's efforts to crack down on critical media facing setbacks in court.

item.title: Atiku’s US Lobbying Against Tinubu Is Self-Defeating, Affront To Nigeria’s Sovereignty — Presidency
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The Presidency has described former Vice-President Atiku Abubakar’s attempt to revive President Bola Tinubu’s decades-old US forfeiture case through foreign lobbyists as self-defeating and an affront to Nigeria’s sovereignty. In a statement issued on Thursday, Presidential Spokesperson Bayo Onanuga

item.title: Tinubu to Northeast leaders: Economy already stabilised, Nigeria in golden era
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: President Bola Tinubu said on Thursday that the consistency in the implementation of economic reforms has stabilised the economy, ushering Nigeria into its golden era, with readiness to enjoy the benefits of democracy. item.title: Goldman Offers Way to Trade AI Junk Bonds $250 Million at a Time
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Goldman Sachs Group Inc. and JPMorgan Chase & Co. launched products this week allowing investors to quickly cut — or ramp up — exposure to tech industry debt, as concerns mount over hyperscalers’ huge future bond sales for artificial intelligence investments.

item.title: Lockheed Martin’s stock skyrockets as push to build more missiles faster pays off
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: Lockheed Martin’s stock jumps as a ramp up in missile production helped lead to an earnings beat and raise.

item.title: The Echo Show 21 is a great smart home hub that’s $80 off
item.dc:creator: The Verge
item.description: Split between buying a smart calendar, a kitchen TV, a smart home hub, and a smart display? Amazon’s Echo Show 21 is all of those things in one, with a huge 21-inch screen. You can use it to control your smart lights, glance at recipes, watch TV shows, and more. Currently, Best Buy and Home […

item.description: Veteran Nollywood actress Ngozi Nwosu has appealed to the public for financial support to undergo multiple life-saving surgeries estimated to cost ₦30 million. The post Veteran Actress Ngozi Nwosu Appeals For ₦30m To Fund Life-Saving Surgeries appeared first on Trending News.

item.description: AMD is challenging its chipmaker rival with a new rack-scale system that will start shipping to customers later this year.

item.title: Wellington Is Passing on Data-Center Debt Deals on Property Value Concerns
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Data-center debt risks are rising, there’s uncertainty over what the properties are worth and returns are falling, according to Wellington Management.

item.title: Japan’s $1.8 trillion pension giant might bring money home. That could jolt U.S. stocks and the Fed.

item.description: Japan’s pension titan selling foreign assets could push U.S. yields up and sap demand for the dollar.

item.dc:creator: TechCrunch
item.description: In a memo to staff that was posted online, Conte said the company's core business is strong, but the platform has to respond to market changes and adjust its cost structure to remain stable.

item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: The Nigerian naira extended its appreciation against the US dollar at the official foreign exchange market on Thursday. Data from the Central Bank of Nigeria,CBN showed that the naira strengthened further to N1,367.76 per dollar on Thursday, up from N1,369.64 per dollar recorded at the official fore

item.dc:creator: Leadership

The Command’s spokesperson SP Josephine Adeh, said in a statement last night that the sus

item.title: Insecurity in Benue will no longer be tolerated – Tinubu

item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: Alphabet says it plans to throw even more money at the AI buildout. The Iran war, oil back at $100 a barrel and rising bond yields make it all the more difficult for the market to stomach.

item.description: Earnings quadrupled on paper, but an analyst says that performance was propped up by unrealized gains from equity investments.

item.title: Details of Peter Obi, British envoy’s meeting emerge
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: The presidential candidate of the Nigeria Democratic Congress (NDC) for the 2027 election, Peter Obi, has disclosed details of his meeting with The post Details of Peter Obi, British envoy’s meeting emerge appeared first on Tribune Online.

item.title: Uniting Divided Bar, Improving Lawyers’ Welfare My Priority – NBA President-Elect
item.dc:creator: TVC News
item.description: The President-elect of the Nigerian Bar Association (NBA), Oyinkansola Badejo-Olusanya (SAN), has identified unity within the legal profession and improved welfare for lawyers as the top priorities of her administration. The post Uniting Divided Bar, Improving Lawyers’ Welfare My Priority – NB

item.title: A ‘generational buying opportunity’ guarantees inflation plus 3% a year, says this hedge-fund manager
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: Bob Elliott sparks buzz highlighting the bargain level of TIPS.

item.title: Court grants Blessing CEO N20m bail over alleged N69.15m fraud
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: Social media personality, Blessing Okoro, popularly known as Blessing CEO, was on Thursday granted N20 million bail by an Ikeja Special Offences Court over alleged N69.15 million property fraud. The post Court grants Blessing CEO N20m bail over alleged N69.15m fraud appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.title: Minnesota man who killed Democratic lawmaker sentenced to life in US prison
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: Judge says that long sentence is merited for slaying of state Democratic lawmaker Melissa Hortman and her husband Mark.

item.title: Imax’s CEO says it’s time to hike prices as tickets to ‘The Odyssey’ resell for $1,000
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: People are flying across the country to see Christopher Nolan’s epic fantasy blockbuster “The Odyssey,” the first full-length movie shot only with Imax cameras.

item.title: 311 Reps members back passage of State Police bill as 15 differ
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: Three hundred and eleven members of the 10th House of Representatives voted in support of the bill seeking to establish State Police Services in The post 311 Reps members back passage of State Police bill as 15 differ appeared first on Tribune Online.

item.title: Youths Lock NVRI Gates As Berom Community Protests FCAHPT Provost’s Appointment
item.dc:creator: Channels TV
item.description: As of press time, neither the Federal Ministry of Agriculture and Food Security nor the college management had issued an official statement regarding the protesters' demands The post Youths Lock NVRI Gates As Berom Community Protests FCAHPT Provost’s Appointment appeared first on Channels Tele

item.title: US Pastor Convicted Of Stealing $340,000 From Churches To Fund Gambling
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: A United States pastor, Dale Sanders, has been convicted of stealing more than $340,000 from two churches in Louisiana and using the money to finance gambling and other personal expenses. The 56-year-old was found guilty by a jury on 25 counts, including wire fraud, access device fraud and obstructi

item.title: FCC Chairman Brendan Carr’s war on the First Amendment
item.dc:creator: The Verge
item.description: As the chairman of the Federal Communications Commission, Brendan Carr has authority over the nation’s TV, radio, and internet. But since Donald Trump was elected to his second term, Carr has wielded that power to threaten broadcasters that have exercised their free speech rights to make jokes about

item.title: Benue recorded one Lassa fever case in one month – Commissioner
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: Benue State Government has announced that only one laboratory-confirmed case of Lassa fever was recorded in the state in the past month, with no fatality, attributing the outcome to sustained disease surveillance, prompt response and improved case management. The post Benue recorded one Lassa fever

item.title: Men these days don’t want to be ‘simps’ anymore — Tyla
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: South African singer, Tyla Laura Seethal, popularly known as Tyla, has shared her views on modern dating, saying many men today are reluctant... The post Men these days don’t want to be ‘simps’ anymore — Tyla appeared first on Tribune Online.

item.title: Tinubu expands Nigerian Army to 12 divisions, approves 28,000 new recruits
item.dc:creator: Nairametrics
item.description: President Bola Tinubu has approved the expansion of the Nigerian Army from eight to 12 divisions, alongside the recruitment of 28,000 additional personnel, as part of efforts to strengthen Nigeria’s security architecture and improve military effectiveness across the country. The post Tinubu expands

item.title: Soldier declared wanted over alleged sale of military uniforms to terrorists
item.dc:creator: Daily Trust
item.description: The Nigerian Army has declared one of its personnel, Private Mohammed Yusuf Amutu, wanted over allegations that he unlawfully sold and supplied military uniforms to terrorists and other criminal elements. The Army disclosed this in a statement issued on Thursday by the Headquarters Nigerian Army Ord

item.title: Nigeria And The New Demands Of Regional Leadership
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: By Ademola Oshodi The international environment is placing a heavier responsibility on Nigeria. Regional security cooperation has weakened, competition for energy finance and technology has intensified, and humanitarian funding is contracting even as domestic needs increase. Three developments this

item.title: Meta drops out of a major clean energy pact as its natural gas buildout accelerates
item.dc:creator: TechCrunch
item.description: Meta has made significant investments in natural gas over the past year. Now it's dropping out of an industry renewable energy group.
